github-devloop decision: approve
Verdicts: teleology=approve parsimony=approve fidelity=approve natural-ownership=approve proportional-containment=approve

teleology:
Under established hermetic, fail-closed toolchain-provenance practice, the natural-ownership peer claim that #111 is mis-scoped does not defeat approval: the proposal targets `resolve_bin_contract` and requires an enforced “host-run invariant,” which aligns with the shared runner’s statement that it “owns BIN resolution” (`.fkst/run/fkst-packages-conformance/scripts/run.sh:12`) and the contract’s ownership of “ALL launch invariants” (`.fkst/run/fkst-packages-conformance/docs/user/control-planes-and-host-repo-composition.md:13`); moreover, this repository has a genuine integration defect because `resolve_testing_bin` bypasses that shared resolver for any executable `BIN` (`scripts/run.sh:103`). The ignored hydrated checkout determines where implementation and dependency-pin updates should land, but that is PR-grounding advice, not evidence that this sound, bounded, code-verifiable issue is false or likely wrong; verification must use the full immutable source pin, not the abbreviated `ENGINE_VER`.

parsimony:
Established hermetic-resolution and SLSA/in-toto provenance practice requires one fail-closed authorization gate that binds an executable to the authoritative immutable source revision. The natural-ownership peer’s claim that the issue is mis-scoped does not defeat approval: although the shared runner says it owns `BIN` resolution and one-host launch invariants (`.fkst/run/fkst-packages-conformance/scripts/run.sh:12`; `.fkst/run/fkst-packages-conformance/docs/user/control-planes-and-host-repo-composition.md:13`), the issue specifies behavior rather than a repository-local implementation, and this repository’s tracked wrapper itself bypasses that shared resolver by accepting any executable `BIN` first (`scripts/run.sh:101`). Development can therefore place the provenance query in `fkst-substrate`, central verification in the shared `resolve_bin_contract`, and the necessary consumption and integration coverage here; ownership is a PR-grounding concern, not an evidenced contradiction or authority failure in the proposal. Advance it, while requiring exact comparison against the full embedded source pin rather than the abbreviated `ENGINE_VER`.

fidelity:
Established SLSA/in-toto practice assigns artifact identity to the producer and centralized fail-closed verification to the launcher. The natural-ownership peer’s mis-scope attack does not defeat approval: the proposal names `resolve_bin_contract` and requires a host-run invariant, while repository source explicitly delegates binary resolution to that shared contract ([scripts/run.sh](scripts/run.sh:101)) and the shared contract declares ownership of `BIN` resolution ([shared run.sh](.fkst/run/fkst-packages-conformance/scripts/run.sh:12)); nothing in the issue mandates duplicating verification downstream. Development should therefore advance through the producer-owned full-revision query, shared resolver gate, removal of this wrapper’s explicit-`BIN` bypass, pin update, and host integration tests. Exact authorization must compare the full embedded source revision; the twelve-character `ENGINE_VER` remains display-only advisory.

natural-ownership:
Under established hermetic toolchain and SLSA/in-toto provenance practice, the exact pinned revision must authorize the executable at one fail-closed launch boundary. The peer claim defeats my ownership objection: the issue expressly names `resolve_bin_contract`, applies one invariant before `check`, `test`, and `supervise`, and therefore targets the shared resolver class rather than prescribing a duplicate consumer-only verifier; current source confirms both the unsafe first-executable selection (`scripts/run.sh:101`, `.fkst/run/fkst-packages-conformance/scripts/bin_bootstrap.sh:65`) and the shared host-run ownership boundary (`.fkst/run/fkst-packages-conformance/docs/user/control-planes-and-host-repo-composition.md:13`). Development is sound, bounded, actionable, and code-verifiable; PR review should require exact full `FKST_FRAMEWORK_SOURCE_PIN` equality and treat the 12-character `ENGINE_VER` only as display, with cross-repository delivery and the eventual pin update handled as implementation coordination rather than an abstain ground.

proportional-containment:
The natural-ownership peer’s claim that the issue is mis-scoped does not establish a blocker: the issue specifies a host-run invariant rather than prescribing a repository-local implementation, and the ownership evidence identifies the proper implementation locus instead of contradicting the proposal. Established hermetic toolchain and software-provenance practice requires exact, fail-closed artifact authorization at the execution boundary; here the shared HOST-RUN contract explicitly owns “ALL launch invariants” including BIN resolution (`.fkst/run/fkst-packages-conformance/docs/user/control-planes-and-host-repo-composition.md:10-14`), while the tracked wrapper invokes that contract but first accepts executable `BIN` without verification (`scripts/run.sh:101-115`), and `resolve_bin_contract` accepts every ambient candidate before consulting the pinned cache (`.fkst/run/fkst-packages-conformance/scripts/bin_bootstrap.sh:60-130`). Thus producer ownership is implementation guidance, not evidence that the issue is false, unsafe, duplicate, or unresolvably ambiguous. Advance the proposal, requiring the shared gate and any necessary producer change to expose the full embedded `FKST_FRAMEWORK_SOURCE_PIN` (`.fkst/run/fkst-substrate-pinned/crates/fkst-framework/build.rs:20-38`); treat the abbreviated `ENGINE_VER` (`.fkst/run/fkst-substrate-pinned/crates/fkst-framework/src/provenance.rs:47-49`) only as display output.

<!-- fkst:github-proxy:comment:implement/comment/implementing-state/ready/consensus-github-devloop/issue/ChronoAIProject/fkst-packages-testing/111/2026-07-19T13-37-19Z -->

> *This was generated by AI during triage.*

Manual implementation is complete on local `dev` as commit `8254b34` (`fix: enforce pinned FKST engine provenance`). Nothing has been pushed.

Implementation summary:
- Added `scripts/engine_provenance.sh` as the enforced engine-resolution gate before host runtime work.
- The authoritative identity is the full SHA in `.fkst/substrate-ref`.
- Provenance is queried hermetically by running `fkst-framework init-package-repo` in a temporary Git repository and reading the embedded full source pin.
- Explicit `BIN` and `.fkst/env` candidates fail closed when provenance is unavailable or mismatched.
- PATH and sibling-checkout candidates are optional: stale binaries are warned about and skipped without modifying their source trees.
- The existing content-addressed cache and pinned-source `bootstrap_bin_on_total_miss` remain the canonical fallback.
- Successful startup exports `FKST_ENGINE_SOURCE_PIN` and normalized 12-character `FKST_ENGINE_VER`, and prints expected pin, observed pin, and selected binary before command execution.

Verification:
- `python3 -B scripts/engine_provenance_test.py`: 9 tests passed, covering matching/stale explicit BIN, CI-injected BIN, `.fkst/env`, stale PATH, dirty sibling preservation, cache hit/build, readonly miss, and unavailable provenance.
- `python3 -B scripts/run_script_contract_test.py`: 15 tests passed; `check`, `test`, and `supervise` assert the startup invariant.
- `./scripts/run.sh check`: passed against the real repository. It observed stale sibling revision `723f5433814975d5c02723e7c5874190c7b8ed73`, left it untouched, and selected the exact cached engine for required revision `e3355b42709f4138613b8238cba34a5ab1161053`.
- Bash syntax, Python compilation, and `git diff --check` passed.

A full `./scripts/run.sh test` was also attempted. The #111 behavior passed, but the run stopped on an unrelated existing `environment-factory` regression: `test_node_runtime_regressions` expected `extra-listener:` and received `listener-missing:<port>` (`72 passed, 1 failed`). This change does not modify that package.

> *This was generated by AI during triage.*

Follow-up validation on July 20, 2026: the previously reported unrelated `environment-factory` failure was a flaky test-fixture race, not a #111 runtime failure.

Root cause:
- the listener test selected ports with `62000 + (pid % 1000)`, allowing collisions under concurrent test execution;
- it used a fixed 250 ms sleep instead of waiting until the child listener had actually bound its sockets.

Local follow-up commit `1e50b69` (`test: remove environment listener race`) now lets the OS allocate free ports, waits for an atomic readiness artifact, and only then evaluates process-group listener ownership.

Verification:
- the original 80-run, 24-way parallel reproducer changed from frequent `listener-missing` / `foreign-listener` failures to 80/80 passing;
- `./scripts/run.sh test environment-factory`: 73 passed, 0 failed;
- full `./scripts/run.sh test`: all 11 packages, repository contract tests, 9 engine provenance tests, 15 runner contract tests, 10 OpenSandbox adapter tests, and the Lua coverage ratchet passed.

The local `dev` branch now contains #111 commit `8254b34` plus the independent test-stability commit `1e50b69`. Nothing has been pushed.

title: P0: Enforce pinned FKST engine provenance before host supervise
